behavior_in_task_description.reason: The instruction explicitly names every variable, function, pattern, and literal string tested: typeOrder, typeLabels, conventionalMatch, groups[type], `### ${label}`, `#### ${pkg.name}`, fs.readFileSync(changelogPath), git log --pretty=format, %h %ae %s, --no-merges, appendAuthors, resolveAuthorForCommit, authorCache, resolveAuthorForPR, prAuthorCache, resolveUsername, typeIndex, sort with typeIndex(a) - typeIndex(b), and all type label mappings like 'Features', 'Fix', etc.

no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction specifies exact implementation details: the exact regex pattern `/^(\w+)(?:\(([^)]*)\))?:\s*(.*)$/`, the exact variable name `conventionalMatch`, exact git log format `--pretty=format:"%h %ae %s"`, exact `typeIndex` function with `typeIndex(a) - typeIndex(b)` sorting, exact `typeOrder` array contents and order, exact `typeLabels` mappings. This leaves almost no reasoning for the agent — it's essentially dictating the solution line by line.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert on gold-specific implementation details: variable name `conventionalMatch` (test_conventional_commit_parsing), `groups[type]` pattern (test_groups_by_commit_type), `typeIndex` function name (test_type_sorting_by_priority), exact `typeOrder` array syntax (test_typeorder_array_exists), exact `typeLabels` object syntax (test_typelabels_object_exists). An alternative correct implementation using different variable names (e.g., `commitMatch`, `categories`, `typePriority`) or a different

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Almost all fail-to-pass tests only read the file and check for string patterns: e.g., `assert 'typeOrder' in content`, `assert 'conventionalMatch' in content`, `assert re.search(r'groups\[type\]', content)`. Only test_script_syntax_valid and test_repo_eslint_scripts/test_repo_prettier_scripts actually execute the code. No test runs the script and checks the generated changelog output.

test_not_tautological.reason: A stub implementation that simply contains the right string literals without any real logic would pass all fail-to-pass tests. For example, adding dead-code comments like `// typeOrder = ['feat','fix','perf','refactor','docs','chore','test','ci']` and `// conventionalMatch` and `// groups[type]` would satisfy the text-matching assertions, as long as the old functions are removed and syntax is valid.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All major behavioral requirements from the instruction are tested: typeOrder, typeLabels, conventional commit parsing, grouping by type, markdown headings, no CHANGELOG.md reading, git log format, removal of appendAuthors/resolveAuthorForCommit/authorCache, type sorting, PR author resolution preservation, and merge commit filtering.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-organized with clear section comments separating 'Fail-to-Pass Tests' and 'Pass-to-Pass Tests', descriptive function names, and docstrings explaining each test's purpose.

structured_data_schema.reason: The task produces a markdown changelog string, not structured data like JSON/CSV/API/DB.

no_typos.reason: No typos found in filenames, paths, commands, or variable names across instruction.md and tests.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: The instruction names the exact file to modify (reasonable for single-file task), but also dictates exact variable names (conventionalMatch, typeOrder, typeLabels, typeIndex, groups), exact regex patterns, exact function signatures to add/remove, and exact sorting logic — going far beyond describing what's broken into prescribing the exact fix.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: This is a code-only task modifying a JavaScript script, not a config edit task.
